questions.question: Which order type guarantees immediate execution but not the exact price?
questions.explanation: A market order executes immediately at the best available price, but the exact price may differ slightly from what you see (slippage).

questions.question: You want to buy a stock only if it drops to $50 or below. Which order type should you use?
questions.explanation: A buy limit order at $50 will only execute if the price reaches $50 or lower, ensuring you don't pay more than your target.

questions.question: What happens when a stop-loss order is triggered?
options: It waits until end of day
questions.explanation: A stop (stop-loss) order becomes a market order once the stop price is reached, executing at the next available price.

questions.question: What does GTC stand for?
questions.explanation: GTC (Good Till Cancelled) means the order stays open until it is either filled or you manually cancel it.
